title: Split Part 5 of the onboarding/rollout flow into two confirmed sub-steps

requirements.summary: Split the frontend canister upgrade step (Part 5) into two distinct sub-steps (Part 5a and Part 5b), each with its own screen, description, and confirmation prompt that must be acknowledged before proceeding
acceptanceCriteria: Part 5 is divided into exactly two sequential sub-steps (Part 5a and Part 5b).
Each sub-step displays its own descriptive content explaining what action is about to be performed.
A confirmation dialog or inline confirmation prompt appears before entering Part 5a, requiring the user to confirm before proceeding.
A second confirmation dialog or inline confirmation prompt appears before entering Part 5b, requiring the user to confirm before proceeding.
Users cannot skip either confirmation — the 'Continue' / 'Proceed' action is disabled until confirmation is given.
The step indicator/progress tracker (if present) reflects the two sub-steps correctly.
Declining or cancelling a confirmation returns the user to the previous state without advancing.

file_operations.description: Update the rollout steps documentation to reflect the split of Part 5 into Part 5a and Part 5b. Each sub-step must have its own numbered section with clear action items and acceptance criteria. Part 5a should cover the preparation phase for the frontend canister upgrade (e.g., verifying build artifacts, confirming deployment readiness). Part 5b should cover the actual frontend canister upgrade execution (e.g., deploying to mainnet, verifying the upgrade). Preserve all existing step content and maintain consistency with the documentation structure used in Steps 1-9.5 and Step 10.

Create a new admin-only multi-step wizard page component for guiding administrators through the rollout process. This component should render a step indicator showing all rollout steps including the newly split Part 5a and Part 5b. Each step should display descriptive content from ROLLOUT_STEPS.md. Implement navigation controls (Back/Next) that are disabled until the current step's confirmation is acknowledged. For Part 5a and Part 5b specifically, include confirmation checkboxes or radio buttons that must be checked before the Next button becomes enabled. Store wizard state (current step, confirmation status) in React state. Use shadcn/ui components (Card, Button, Checkbox, Alert) for consistent styling. Ensure the wizard is only accessible to admin users.

Create a reusable confirmation dialog component for rollout step confirmations. Accept props for title, description, confirmationText, onConfirm, and onCancel callbacks. Use shadcn/ui AlertDialog component. Display a checkbox labeled with the confirmationText prop that must be checked before the Confirm button becomes enabled. Include Cancel and Confirm buttons with appropriate styling (Cancel as ghost variant, Confirm as default). Call onCancel if the dialog is closed without confirming. This dialog will be used by RolloutWizardPage for both Part 5a and Part 5b confirmation prompts.

Add a new route '/portal/admin/rollout-wizard' for the RolloutWizardPage component. Place this route under the admin section alongside existing admin routes like '/portal/admin/dashboard' and '/portal/admin/roles'. Wrap the route with AuthGate to ensure only authenticated users can access it. The route should be registered in the TanStack Router route tree structure following the existing pattern.

Add a navigation item for 'Rollout Wizard' in the sidebar navigation array. The item should have icon: Settings, label: 'Rollout Wizard', href: '/portal/admin/rollout-wizard', and adminOnly: true. Position it in the admin section of the navigation array after the existing admin items (Dashboard, Roles, Bootstrap). Ensure the navigation item is filtered based on the user's admin role using the existing isAdmin check.

Define TypeScript types for the rollout wizard state management. Include a RolloutStep interface with fields: id (string), title (string), description (string), requiresConfirmation (boolean), confirmationText (optional string), isCompleted (boolean). Define a RolloutWizardState interface with fields: currentStepIndex (number), steps (RolloutStep[]), confirmations (Map<string, boolean>). Export these types for use in RolloutWizardPage and related components.

Create a custom React hook for managing rollout wizard state. Export a useRolloutWizard hook that returns: currentStep (RolloutStep), allSteps (RolloutStep[]), canProceed (boolean), goToNextStep (function), goToPreviousStep (function), confirmCurrentStep (function), resetWizard (function). Initialize the steps array with all rollout steps including Part 5a and Part 5b as separate entries. Implement logic to enable/disable navigation based on confirmation status. Use useState for local state management. This hook will be consumed by RolloutWizardPage to manage wizard flow.
